topic: In China employee termination: legal-gate decision tree for foreign employers
primary_keyword: China employee termination decision tree foreign employers
related_keywords: China Labor Contract Law termination routes

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Core source for route logic and termination consequence framework in decision-tree branching.

sources.title: Regulations for the Implementation of the Labor Contract Law of the PRC (State Council Decree No. 535)
sources.fit_note: Supports branch-level implementation and process interpretation.

sources.title: Interpretation of the Supreme People's Court on Issues Concerning the Application of Law in the Trial of Labor Dispute Cases (I)
sources.fit_note: Adds dispute-lens checkpoints for evidence and consistency nodes.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.fit_note: Anchors offboarding contribution closure checks in final branch execution.

sources.title: Employee Termination in China: A Guide for Employers
sources.fit_note: Provides practical branch interpretation for foreign-reader usability.

source_replacement_notes: Rotated from myth-vs-fact to decision-tree format; retained legal-hierarchy sources for stability.
Excluded broad comparative/tax-dominant sources because branch logic is legal-route centric.
Kept China Briefing as practical translator for foreign operators at each branch.
consensus: Decision quality improves when legal route gates are explicit before operational execution.
Evidence sufficiency and cross-function consistency are key go/no-go branch conditions.
Foreign employers need plain-language route branching to avoid at-will assumptions.
uncertainties: City-level implementation and evidence weighting may vary.
Case-specific payment component treatment may require local confirmation.
Official English texts may not reflect every Chinese-language practice nuance.
source_fit_assessment: Sufficient for decision-tree format because sources support legal branching logic plus practical implementation interpretation.
